root_text.ud2.2:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.
root_text.ud2.2:1.3: Tena kho pana samayena sambahulānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ pacchābhattaṁ piṇḍapātapaṭikkantānaṁ upaṭṭhānasālāyaṁ sannisinnānaṁ sannipatitānaṁ ayamantarākathā udapādi:
root_text.ud2.2:1.4: “ko nu kho, āvuso, imesaṁ dvinnaṁ rājūnaṁ mahaddhanataro vā mahābhogataro vā mahākosataro vā mahāvijitataro vā mahāvāhanataro vā mahabbalataro vā mahiddhikataro vā mahānubhāvataro vā rājā vā māgadho seniyo bimbisāro, rājā vā pasenadi kosalo”ti?
root_text.ud2.2:1.5: Ayañcarahi tesaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ antarākathā hoti vippakatā.
root_text.ud2.2:2.1: Atha kho bhagavā sāyanhasamayaṁ paṭisallānā vuṭṭhito yenupaṭṭhānasālā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā paññatte āsane nisīdi.
root_text.ud2.2:2.3: “kāya nuttha, bhikkhave, etarahi kathāya sannisinnā sannipatitā, kā ca pana vo antarākathā vippakatā”ti?
root_text.ud2.2:3.1: “Idha, bhante, amhākaṁ pacchābhattaṁ piṇḍapātapaṭikkantānaṁ upaṭṭhānasālāyaṁ sannisinnānaṁ sannipatitānaṁ ayamantarākathā udapādi:
root_text.ud2.2:3.2: ‘ko nu kho, āvuso, imesaṁ dvinnaṁ rājūnaṁ mahaddhanataro vā mahābhogataro vā mahākosataro vā mahāvijitataro vā mahāvāhanataro vā mahabbalataro vā mahiddhikataro vā mahānubhāvataro vā rājā vā māgadho seniyo bimbisāro, rājā vā pasenadi kosalo’ti?
root_text.ud2.2:3.3: Ayaṁ kho no, bhante, antarākathā vippakatā, atha bhagavā anuppatto”ti.
root_text.ud2.2:4.1: “Na khvetaṁ, bhikkhave, tumhākaṁ patirūpaṁ kulaputtānaṁ saddhā agārasmā anagāriyaṁ pabbajitānaṁ yaṁ tumhe evarūpiṁ kathaṁ katheyyātha.
root_text.ud2.2:4.3: dhammī vā kathā ariyo vā tuṇhībhāvo”ti.
root_text.ud2.2:5.1: Atha kho bhagavā etamatthaṁ viditvā tāyaṁ velāyaṁ imaṁ udānaṁ udānesi:

translation_text.ud2.2:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
translation_text.ud2.2:1.3: Now at that time, after the meal, on return from almsround, several mendicants sat together in the assembly hall and this discussion came up among them:
translation_text.ud2.2:1.4: “Which of these two kings has greater wealth, riches, treasury, dominion, vehicles, forces, might, and power: King Seniya Bimbisāra of Magadha or King Pasenadi of Kosala?”
translation_text.ud2.2:1.5: At that point the conversation among those mendicants was left unfinished.
translation_text.ud2.2:2.1: Then in the late afternoon, the Buddha came out of retreat, went to the assembly hall, sat down on the seat spread out,
translation_text.ud2.2:2.3: “Mendicants, what were you sitting talking about just now? What conversation was left unfinished?”
translation_text.ud2.2:3.1: So the mendicants told him what they had been talking about when the Buddha arrived. The Buddha said,
translation_text.ud2.2:4.1: “Mendicants, it is not appropriate for you gentlemen who have gone forth out of faith from the lay life to homelessness to talk about such things.
translation_text.ud2.2:4.2: When you’re sitting together you should do one of two things:
translation_text.ud2.2:4.3: discuss the teachings or keep noble silence.”
translation_text.ud2.2:5.1: Then, understanding this matter, on that occasion the Buddha expressed this heartfelt sentiment:
translation_text.ud2.2:6.3: is worth even a sixteenth part
translation_text.ud2.2:6.4: of the happiness of craving’s end.”

reference_text.ud2.2:1.1: mc0.106, ms18Ud_78, msdiv12, ndp20.74, pts-vp-pli11, sya25.87
